title: Reinforcement Learning, second edition: An Introduction ...
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Purchase options and add-ons
Content:
The significantly expanded and updated new edition of a widely used text on reinforcement learning, one of the most active research areas in artificial intelligence.
Reinforcement learning, one of the most active research areas in artificial intelligence, is a computational approach to learning whereby an agent tries to maximize the total amount of reward it receives while interacting with a complex, uncertain environment. In Reinforcement Learning , Richard Sutton and Andrew Barto provide a clear and simple account of the field's key ideas and algorithms. This second edition has been significantly expanded and updated, presenting new topics and updating coverage of other topics.

title: Pioneering AI researcher to advise RBC's machine learning lab | CBC News
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > 'The father of reinforcement learning'
Content:
Yann LeCun , Facebook's Director of AI, called Sutton "the father of reinforcement learning" [in a Facebook post last March](https://www.facebook.com/yann.lecun?lst=507465696%3A722677142%3A1484677583) .
In fact, Sutton wrote the book, publishing the first edition of the textbook *[Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction](https://mitpress.mit.edu/books/reinforcement-learning)* in 1998 with his co-author and former academic advisor Andrew Barto .
Sebastian Thrun — himself an early pioneer in AI and self-driving cars, who later co-founded Google's X lab for research and development efforts in 2007 — [reviewed the book shortly after its publication](http://www.aaai.org/ojs/index.php/aimagazine/article/view/1501) , calling it a worthy read for AI novices and veterans alike.

title: Richard S. Sutton
excerpts: **Richard Stuart Sutton** FRS FRSC (born 1957 or 1958) is a Canadian computer scientist . He is a professor of computing science at the University of Alberta , fellow & Chief Scientific Advisor at the Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute , and a research scientist at Keen Technologies. <sup>[[ 1 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> Sutton is considered one of the founders of modern computational reinforcement learning . <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> In particular, he contributed to temporal difference learning and policy gradient methods . <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> He received the 2024 Turing Award with Andrew Barto . <sup>[[ 9 ]](:0-9)</sup> <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup>

title: The man who taught AI to learn believes human-level intelligence is ...
excerpts:
Section Title: The man who taught AI to learn believes human-level intelligence is closer than you think > Author
Content:
[Sascha Brodsky](https://www.ibm.com/think/author/sascha-brodsky)
Staff Writer
IBM
Richard Sutton, one of the pioneers behind modern artificial intelligence, is not convinced that simply throwing more computing power at AI will lead to machines that think like humans. In fact, he argues today’s obsession with scaling up deep learning might be holding AI back from its full potential.
Sutton, alongside his longtime collaborator Andrew Barto, [won this year’s Turing Award](https://amturing.acm.org/byyear.cfm) —often called the "Nobel Prize of Computing"—for his work in [reinforcement learning](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/reinforcement-learning) . He believes the real breakthrough will come when AI stops relying on curated datasets and starts learning from experience, much like a child does.

Content:
The document is about the second edition of the book 'Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction' by Richard S. Sutton and Andrew G. Barto, published by The MIT Press. It includes details about the book's content, licensing, and publication information, as well as a list of chapters covering various aspects of reinforcement learning. The book is part of the Adaptive Computation and Machine Learning series and is available for download on BuyUBooks.com.
